reviews.review_text: Its fine, It does not feel like Halo, even the controls are different and legacy is not true legacy for the original trilogy you still need to modify them more. open world makes it none linear like past games. probably wont finish it I am gonna go play Halo 3

Another example of nostalgia poisoning among the fanbase. Halo: Infinite is a fine game - the multiplayer is fun, engaging, and feels like Halo. The gunplay is solid and punchy and exactly what you would expect. It feels like a game with passion behind it on the dev team, though with some heavy mismanagement from Microsoft plaguing it for a good few years. 

As it stands now, the game is in a much better state than it was. But as always, people live off of outrage that doesn't matter. Take any review that uses provocative language with a heavy grain of salt; these people don't actually care beyond needing something to be mad at.

Launch had tons of hype but got fumbled pretty hard without enough content.

As of now? 2026? It's actually in a pretty good state, and while there's definitely still concerns like the always-online demand and such. It's a good halo game, and worth checking out.

My first experience with Halo was seeing the Apple showcase of their products and this game that would be coming out. It instantly hooked me with a space scifi gameplay and setting. Later when I picked up the Original Xbox with a stack of games around 2004 I played through the first Halo over and over until I moved over to Halo 2. I played that game so much I kept on replaying the mission where you can get the Scarab Gun by flying a Banshee just because I wanted too try and find more secrets even replaying every level and doing dumb stuff. In High School I remember all of the trailers and commercials for Halo 3, it was just a moment in time if you weren't alive or watching you missed out. Didn't get a chance too get a 360 until 2010 when Halo Reach came out as well picked it up with a couple of games including Fable 3.

After that time period I kept replaying the original Halo classics over and over a couple times a year. Eventually down the line Halo 4 came out I played it first on the 360 and it wasn't bad but wasn't good. I attempted too give Halo 5 a chance and it was the first Halo game I put down the controller and didn't want anything to do with this. Some years later Xbox Series X was rolling out and Halo Infinite was being showcased I was hoping this time they could do something and fix the series...

Halo 5 & Infinite are a mockery of the Halo Universe and Lore that was built up over the many years and they had a chance to do something with the merging of the Halo Wars and Book lore into the main story. They royally screwed it up and if you want too play Halo, get the Master Chief Collection and Halo Wars Series. Your choice on which games you want too ignore or choose not to exist. But I cannot recommend Halo Infinite or Halo 5 to me they are a mockery of the original games.

Thank you for taking the time too read my long winded review, I hope this helps you and I hope you have a good day.
This sucks, The game is stuck on login page... won't budge whatever you do

I wouldn't recommend spending your hard earned money on this game as it's just going to lock you out at some point after investing a lot of hours into this game. idk what is going on but i haven't played in a long time and when i finally wanna play again it go through all of the booting and loading just to loc me out with a " please log into your Xbox account" without actually letting me do that. i mean I am logged into the xbox app but when it gives me this message the closes i got to being able to "log in" was going through a security check where it made me prove i wasn't a robot and sent my phone a security code. i did both and then it locked me into an endless loop of giving me a button that says "okay" then takes me to a scrren with only a button that says "log in" then tells me there was an issue and to "log in" i press "okay" and back to the screen that that only has a button that says "log in" and around and around i f'in go. i tried clearing credentials and logging out and logging in on steam and xbox restarting my computer. everything outside of uninstalling and reinstalling games and apps. i currently do not have the data to do that as i have in laws staying with us eating up all of my data.... -_- furthermore that's not something i should have to do. if we're gonna play this own nothing and like it b.s. i expect things to work ALL of the time EVERY TIME! i'd rather go back to physical disks and F steam, and microsoft and every other greedy af company that can't ever seem to get things right it's not my job to troubleshoot and make things work..this kinda b.s. is making me rethink buying a new computer to game more if all i'm gonna be met with is nonsense and b.s. like this.

The longest load time for the game to start EVER! very few game modes or active players half the lobbies are filled with bots. Forge is cool though, MCC is still way better. I'd suggest Halo 5 over this even. Paying for cosmetics on Halo is a scam, at least in Halo 5 you got things that helped you in matches when you bought rec packs.

Id say halo infinites campain is a decent where there were some parts where it was just walking to certain places, The animations are clean and love the grapple hook but it has to much cinamatic cutscenes and not enough action scenes.  but the transtion from gameplay the cutscenes were pretty good also the mutliplayer is pretty fun the one thing i wish you can do is view some cutscenes when having them unlocked instead of having to replay the whole misson. would recommend if you like free roam though. Also the master chief deisign in infinite might be my favorite design.

I started with Halo classic on Xbox  through to Halo Infinite.  I really have enjoyed the franchise games and also the game play.  I really recommend this game because of its iconic place in history and the replayability of the game!!
Most of the game is good, terrible ending. Story was pretty sloppy.
idc what people say this game (multiplayer and the campaign) slaps
Servers don't work, even after finding a workaround for the loading issue I still couldn't connect to a lobby. Don't care if it's a skill issue, I have plenty of other games to play that do work.

I thought I'd hate this game despite not liking Halo 4 because of the Prometheans and growing up with the Bungie era, but Halo Infinite was great imo. I really like the characters, the story it extends on, and overall a really fun multiplayer that I'm terrible at. I like this game.

The multiplayer has gotten a massive glowup overtime where it's far worth playing now than it did in it's first year with the six-month gap of content due to unfortunate corporate meandering (yet again) and the game suffered as a result and shares that legacy today because of it which is a shame because it is truly a really fun game and what the team did here pushing the updates is impressive despite all odds going against it. PvE modes, Forge, less FOMO, it's great. 

Campaign however I don't share the same feelings on mainly because it's only the setup for something we never got and midway through the game loses steam with the repetition hitting in so at the end replaying I was left feeling nothing, but it is still worth playing because there are strong moments and the combat they nailed it, but do not get it at full price and keep your expectations in check, maybe even grab a friend or two.

Halo Infinite is a good game despite everything going against it.

Oh and do not install hi-res textures otherwise performance tanks and loading times extend to almost 15 minutes worth at least from my experience.

It is an ok Halo game. Get it on sale. The multiplayer was fixed and feels like Halo 3 but new, and frankly if the MP side of the game had launched in the state its currently in, I believe Halo Infinite MP would go down as amazing but alas... Corporate greed won again lol.

The Single player is alright as well, feels like Halo 1 and 3 mixed together. The fights are fun, the open world isn't bad, I honestly had a lot of fun with the bosses as well, but *spoiler alert* the new enemies they introduce near the end are sooooooo bad and no flood returning. 343 or now Halo Studios cannot make a good story to save their lives lol.

If you are a fan of the series and like Halo campaigns pick this up on sale. Coop can be fun too! If you got any enjoyment from H4 or H5: Guardians you will like Infinite a lot. Otherwise there is nothing wrong with skipping it.

If you are a MP fan this game is honestly really fun but the lower player count means very sweaty lobbies.
I actually enjoyed the story but the open world wasn't that great. I had no interest in the multiplayer tbh. The story wasn't bad though. It wasn't amazing but it was worth a play through.

I have loved Halo ever since I was young. Some of my best gaming memories come from Halo. The sad thing is, I just cannot recommend this game. 

The multiplayer is full of mega-sweats. If you do good in even 1 game, you get put into a lobby with at least 2 other enemy players who are in a stack and a higher rank than you. Instead of rewarding you for doing good, it feels like the game punishes you for trying to be a decent player. This also extends towards spartan customization. You need at least 100,000 SP (Spartan Points) to be able to afford any decent armor sets from the store. You need to grind hours upon hours just to be able to get the full armor you want. I would argue that it's even worse than Halo Reach's grind, solely on the fact that playing the multiplayer feels miserable at times.

The campaign also feels... off. Story-wise and gameplay-wise, it just doesn't feel like a Halo story. There are definitely story beats where old Halo bleeds through, but they are far and few between. 

Overall, I would rate Halo Infinite at a 3.0/10.
There just isn't of a reason for me to stay.

Halo Infinite is an extremely hard title to rate in all honesty, I've bled Halo my entire life and I remember the night I played Halo Infinite's campaign for the first time when it came out, I was pretty blown away with how fun the gameplay mechanics were but the story itself was extremely underwhelming and full of plot holes, the books that have come out semi fill those plot holes up now but at the time the story made absolutely no sense. There are some really good emotional moments and grabs in the story, but over all it doesn't necessarily feel like Halo while also still feeling like Halo, it's a strange feeling. The multiplayer is fun, there's a pretty active community and the game runs and performs great, It's a long way from where it was when I had first played it and it felt absolutely empty and devoid of content. Overall Halo Infinite is a fun Halo game but the story and vibes of everything are super weak in comparison to almost any other Halo title, we don't even get games anymore I have to read books to keep up on the Halo lore it's absolutely crazy how far Halo has fallen for what it is. I did meet the voice actor for Echo-216 though and got a custom Blind Skull signed by him and he said that he had an incredible time working on the game and it was his favorite project he has ever worked on so take that as you will. Verdict; Get the game on sale like you would for the Master Chief Collection.
Terrible menus that can load for several minutes. Everything else is good
Good Multiplayer even if it took a year or two after launch to get a actual decent amount of content and content stream. Great game but seemed to little to late for halo's not so triumphant return.

Very overhated, this is coming from someone who has played since the beta on november. 
Campaign story is a little wishy washy but the elements are nice and the open world is both pretty and engaging, especially with skulls (game modifiers). It is also wonderfully detailed. 
Multiplayer is in a good state nowadays especially since the exchange is fully expanded, but during the early days was definitley worth the criticism. 
This games sandbox is probably the most fun of any of the halo games though! The movement is clean and fun, the guns feel satisfying and the gameplay depth is excellent. 
In modern lobbies since the playerbase has fell off you will probably run into some extremely skilled players, but there is moderate sbmm (which I personally dislike but should help protect new players from occurances of that happening too often, except big team battle ofc.) 
Overall reccomend! 
multiplayer at this point: 9/10, only negative is that you need to know what battle passes you should buy to get your money back before getting one that doesnt, (do the first 2 before any of the others) 
Campaign is an 7.7/10 for me, probably higher than most people but i find the gameplay to just be extremely enjoyable. Many story elements like escherams character and the story of the lost spartan squad are quite engaging, and the story really only falls off by the time you get to the sequence, as thats when the pilots character arc completes. picks back up a little when the weapon gets mad at you but overall those later levels (until the road) are just a drag. but still, the open world, weapon modification system, and modularity are probably not worth the high price tag but just get the campaign on sale lol

Still very fun, has an enjoyable multiplayer, and 343 cooked with the audio design, imo. I was pleasantly surprised with how much i enjoy playing it despite initial negative preconceptions - if you want H3 go play H3. Haven't had this much genuine fun with Halo since Reach, and it feels like a solid direction to take Halo while staying faithful to the original experience, I am not disappointed!
Lindo, lento, asqueroso PvE y los jefes en legendario son casi como el Halo 2 en normal.

I love Halo and they completely botched this one. Note... I play Halo for the campaign, not the multiplayer. I couldn't even finish this one. They went from badass story immersion to a drool fest open-world. Plenty of beautiful scenery to look at and some fortress raiding, squad rescuing and formidable enemy hunting but after playing over 70 hours I just got bored and put it down. I've tried picking it back up more than a couple times but again, got bored. This, in my opinion, is a crap Halo game.

Hopped back in to try some of the new firefight stuff, went to customize my spartan, only to find out that
1: The helmet that I've been suing since reach is only available in a bundle that I have to pay for
2: The other helmet I use is has the attachment "vaulted" for it
The gameplay is fine, but the store trying to Nickle and dime me for basic cosmetics is frustrating and that's probably not going to change anytime soon if people keep buying them.
The firefight mode was, eh anyways. Its a basic, "Enemies spawn from a gate, you kill them" there not much more to it than that.

This game is what you get if you took a beloved franchise put it in a blender and then tried to put the pieces back together, like yeah its still that beloved franchise but things are missing and it was a mess in the beginning.

I am 39 years old and new to the Halo series. I absolutely adore this game. I am glad I didn't play the other ones when I was younger. If you don't give a crap about the context of a game series, it is easier to just enjoy it for what it is: a really cool game.
False advertising the game is not infinite you could prob beat it in a couple days

Haven't played Halo 5 but don't need to. So many times on legendary I'd die randomly to nothing, get yeeted into the air to the stratosphere to the smallest thing, fly around when I take fatal damage, LOSE CHECKPOINT PROGRESS especially on the final boss, boss hp is inflated and isn't fun dealing with tank bosses that one shot you with alot of different skills. This game could have been good but with characters I struggled to like. nah fam and it didn't come with the base halo infinite either.

I have played FPS since the 90's (incluse every game in halo franchaise) and halo infinite is one of the worst.
Campaign is a failure to reboot the franchise. Same story different enemy. Where is the creativity of the original Halo CE? 
And don't let me start talking about the multiplayer
Every game is predetermined. Who win who lose, everything is set even before it start.
Halfway through every fiesta game, weapon loadout and response changed. And every games always ended with 4x/50
The system is so authoritian and intervene so much it takes the joy out of gaming.
I really want to have something nice to say and not to discourage the developer, but I have none.

Campaign was fun, online use to be fun, with over 3k hours played. I have noticed the last couple months, the team mates I get are clueless and I always go against try hards. It use to be kinda 60/40 split in a teams skill capability, now the game is more like 5/95. I have to get sweaty just to beat people that are average at best because my team mates cant stop going 1-10, 1-15 etc.....

Spoilers - some spoilers

Forget what all the negative haters reviewing on this game are spewing... This Halo is straight crack. It should be called Halo Crack... I was skeptical and had it in my wishlist for a discount day and bought it reduced. And oh man... its halo at its peak. The campaign story is comendable and the effort put into creating an open world section of the halo ring with campaign missions and open world side missions too was a breath of fresh air. The game has taken everything good from the previous five installments and added more layers of depth and expansion on the already action packed and addictive play style, maps, enviroment, graphics, immersion. When you do play the missions you get hints of previous Halos' especially halo ce which I love to see again and in this new age of graphics and tech its an incredibly well polished final product. I played the campaign first on normal and its was challenging at times but not overly so and my first play through was not perfect but I adapted and learn quickly. 
I would advise those new to the game to youtube a few hints and tips just to help one manage the expanded play style with its new features. 
I love the all the weapons, the sounds, the affects, the visuals, the smoothness... mind blowing! 
The enemy AI and voice overs are original and went back to how things were before Halo 4.
The world and enviroments in 4k and breathtaking and I loved the transitions from day to night and seeing up into the heavens made me really feel like I was out in space.
The story explores and expands on the connection between John and his previous version of Cortana and the new version. It has its meaning and message but not all like this and thats okay, the story is not going to appeal to everyone but those hating on it are just pure hate filled people looking to bash a game for no good reason at all. They mostly complain of the different structure of game play and lack of urgency... Buts that to me is no justification to not recommend the game. Its different, the combat style is slightly more laid back as it would be in a real world combat sitaution where humanity is recovering and reforming after taking a loss on the battlefield. 
The multiplayer is well structured, great community, no site of hackers, great maps... its staight crack and I loved every moment win or lose. 
The minds that went into making this game really love this universe, you can see the passion and calculation and the execution is just a pure delight to behold, its a masterpiece and deserves praise.
Those on the ropes buy it, full price or discount its worth it itf your looking for a nostalgic, heartwarming and mindblowing modern take on one of the best stories and gaming worlds ever made.  

Things I would like to see improve in future -
Better hints and instructions and tutorials
A story which leans back more towards the style of Halo 2 where play as both human and covenant characters. 
An expansion similar to ODST or Reach which shows the same story or prequels or expansions from different perspectives giving different play styles and exploration to be experienced.

One of my most favoured halo campaigns (Behind ODST, 3, and Reach.) with a great storyline.

Multiplayer is alright ig.

Campaign is half-baked and incomplete. Don’t bother unless you’re a halo fan and really want to know what happens (spoiler: nothing). Multiplayer is active but it’s a microtransaction Hell. Looking forward to the 4th remaster of halo 1.

Worst Halo ever end of story..
I dont what kind of half ass story this was but dam. Its bad. JUst like the idea to make halo open world. (Failed) Gameplay is ok ish I would have rather gone back and played halo 5 again. This is a disgrace to the halo name.

brand new computer, not top of the line but the closest financially appropriate model. settings on low, have attempted to complete the same mission 6 times. i get 20 minutes in and as soon as i see the AI in the conservatory, it stalls out. same place, tried doing other missions with no problem, the chiefs ai wont shut up about it and keeps demanding we go back to the conservatory. I can't figure out whats going on, i dont see it being a crash, because nothing crashes, it just stalls.

This isn't Halo... yes it has Master chief and a vague sense of the style but in my opinion that's where it ends.

When it comes to campagin, they've lost touch with that edge of grit here and the weight of life. Everything feels too ... smooth? Design wise? It's just off putting and I can't place my finger on it. You don't feel like the dark lone super soldier hero who will save humanity anymore. You're just the misunderstood tall quiet guy in the corner now who with the help of his friends and quirkly AI girl in his head that beats the bad guys. <insert rainbow here as the credits roll>

As for competative... I don't really play it much. It was fun for a bit to kinda run here and there but it's trying too hard to be like all other shooters of today. Buy the cat ear armor and UwU your way through matches that feel like something good from the past that's laying beneath the surface.

Microsoft, 343, Halo Studios... please just ... idk message someone who likes original style of Halo. We can find you that gripping story line again filled with the Hoorah! of the soldiers staring in awe as the 7ft super soldier kicks ass and saves them. The sacrifices made to defend humanity as they battle the unknown and unlock the secrets of anicent civilaztions long gone in a war against the flood.

This just isn't the way it should be for this fanchise. It's depressing.
always love halo the cutoms and building new things and the campaign has the same story line

John Halo is the story of Johnny 117 and his best friend The Artist Formerly Known as Cortana. Their journey begins on the mighty UNSC starship The Pickle of Rick as it exits slipperyspace next door to an enormous Heinz Loop. They crash land on the hoop after being attacked by an aggressive alien group known as the Convychonk. Johnny 117 and The Artist Formerly Known as Cortana then begin their quest to visit several convenient locations on the hoop to find a thing and then another thing then do a third thing before heading back to The Pickle of Rick as it goes Krakatoa.
awesome campaign, awesome firefight, but the two plaer one is better. Great customization, overpriced microtransactions
As a guy who is a fan of Halo all the way from Combat Evolved... I love and recommend this game!

-Type the password again, double checking its 100% correct

-Fine I'll reset my password and re-login to all my websites
-Press and hold button to verify you're not a bot

I am a long time fan of halo. I played the trilogy, I participated in clans, and i love ranked. but infinite has turned my love into bitter hate. No matter what game mode you play there's trolls, people who instead of play the objective or try to get kills, instead stand around and try to rage bait people. The clan community that was prevolent and still somewhat is on reach is dead and gone here. Finally ranked, i remember when i got into halo 5 heavy and did ranked. I'm not going to say that skill based matchmaking isn't needed, because it is needed in ranked. thats the whole reason its ranked. but the last thing i wanna do is get 40 kills in btb, turn around go to ranked and get bent over backwards because for some odd reason lucid is in my lobby. I'll be glad to see this dumpster fire get put out, when 7 comes out. OH WAIT!!! they'll probably pull the same crap again!!!!

Even if you are a die-hard Halo fan, I only recommend buying the campaign if you absolutely need to see it for yourself. The gunplay is solid, but the open world ruins any pacing, or structure the game might otherwise have.

It's like 343 decided "after a couple of flops, we really need to put something good out. We had great luck with that Halo wars storyline. Especially the Halo Wars 2. That Atriox character was great!". Then decided they wanted to just hint at that, have Master Chief on a redemption ark after losing the Infinity, and explore a new Halo. Great in concept, poor in execution. 

Halo Infinite to me was an abolute Hail Mary. 343 had dropped the ball with Halo 4 & 5 deciding to take the CoD approach than understand what Halo was about. Halo was and is supposed to be about a super soldier exploring an alien world that can destroy the galaxy as we know it. It is like Aliens in the aspect that its a timeless piece. Humanity exploring the unknown horrors of the universe. A genocidal religious group waging their campaign against humanity, and an unknown adversary with a primordial and unknown origin predating the Covenant. That's what Halo was about. Humanities' struggle against these other-worldly forces. 

Halo Infinite had the potential to revive the series, where the first two games had dropped the ball. Unfortunately, 343 (now "Halo Studios") dropped the ball again. They had the potential to put humanity against these existential odds and foes, like we grew up with. Bringing in a new generation of players that could go back to the old games and understand some struggles, but start fresh in a storyline of the struggles of humanity taking their place in the cosmos.

We didn't need the stereotypical, "He's the chosen one" story line. We didn't need "Humanity can now keep up with the alien foes with our superior weapons" story line. We wanted Chief to be protecting humanity through and through. 

Chief could have met up with Arbiter and talked about the past on Zeta Halo. That would have intrigued new players to play the old games. We could have had a parallel storyline with Atriox reminicing about his time in the Covenant and had a "Remember No Russian" kind of mission with him going against the humans. THAT WOULD HAVE BEEN AWESOME. 

Halo Studios (yeah I know you did the name-change to save you rears as you try to mine the IP that you didn't lay the foundation for), has no idea what they want to do with Halo. They're desperate to grasp any straws they can get with a commercial success to justify this once great franchise that they now possess. 

I would love Halo to come back in full force. I would love some exploration of the Primordials and the horrors beyond the Galactic Rim. I would love a Halo Horror Game. I just think with this entry and the leadership at 343 Industries, that the hopes of myself and the faithful behind the series, will never be met. 


TL;DR: Don't buy this game if you're looking for free co-op. Let the franchise rest for a decade or two. It like Chief, needs to go in cryosleep. 


TL;DR2: Chief is still asleep after escaping the Ark in Halo 3. Play Reach, ODST, and 1-3. Love you and have a great day.
